behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (request-builder -> API client payload), not just isolated parsing logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network behavior is required to catch this; a capturing in-process fake client is enough, so not System. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: does not depend on model judgment and has no multi-agent sequence, so not Agentic/Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is only about request content ('includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels'), not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module to a fake provider client that records outbound requests. Invoke classification with a known taxonomy and assert the emitted request payload contains every allowed label exactly as configured.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is normalization/validation logic on returned data and can fail within one local function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: correctness here is deterministic mapping ('not in taxonomy' -> 'unknown'), not model quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on output mapping rule, and no cross-module state handoff is required to expose that rule bug.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/response-mapping function with a taxonomy and an out-of-taxonomy provider label (e.g., 'medical') and assert the returned value is exactly 'unknown'.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is pure determinism/idempotence of normalization logic within one local boundary. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: external infra is unnecessary and would add noise. LLM-DEP: not testing model judgment, only deterministic post-processing.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization twice (or many times) with byte-identical raw provider response fixtures and the same taxonomy; assert all outputs are identical.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior explicitly depends on repeated live model outputs and consistency characteristics of the provider decision; stubs cannot reveal this. REAL-INFRA exists, but the dominant failure boundary is model-output behavior, so Agentic over System. MULTI-STEP: repeated calls are independent checks, not a chained agent pipeline, so not Workflow. MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID evidence: behavior requires 'repeated live API calls' and compares returned labels. Boundary signal: correctness is about model decision consistency, not wrapper retries/routing. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: an Integration test with fixed stubbed labels always passes and cannot detect provider-level drift/inconsistency. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text does not describe sequential planner/tool/critic handoffs, only repeated single-step classifications.
behaviors.test_description: Run N live classifications (same doc + taxonomy) against the real provider under controlled prompt/version settings, record raw responses and normalized labels, and fail if normalized label changes while raw provider decision semantics are unchanged.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure typically appears at handoff between token-budget policy and dispatch path (e.g., policy computed but not applied before send). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed; in-process fake provider can assert whether dispatch happened and with what payload. LLM-DEP: does not rely on model output content. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is explicitly 'before dispatch' policy enforcement, which is testable without real network.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget, pass an oversized document, run through the module with a fake provider client, and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) is applied before any outbound call, including payload length/token estimate constraints.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA + LLM-DEP: this depends on real provider-reported usage from an actual AI API call; mocks hide tokenizer/provider accounting differences and always miss real drift. Integration-BRAKE(b): yes, execution path goes through real AI API behavior, so do not keep Integration. MULTI-STEP: single call validation, so not Workflow. MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID evidence: behavior asserts 'provider-reported token usage' within budget. Boundary signal: correctness hinges on external model/provider accounting, not only local preflight estimate code. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Integration with stubbed usage numbers cannot catch real provider tokenization/version changes that breach budget. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because while real HTTP is involved, the critical risk is AI-provider token accounting behavior tied to model endpoint semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification calls for representative 'typical' documents against the real provider, collect returned usage metadata, and assert each call's reported tokens are <= configured budget; fail on any over-budget response.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is retry/backoff orchestration across module and provider client boundary. REAL-INFRA: real network is not required to expose logic bugs; a fake client returning rate-limit then success reproduces failure. LLM-DEP: independent of model output quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about handling a signaled rate-limit condition and retry policy, which is fully reproducible with deterministic in-process fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake provider that returns a rate-limit error on first call and success on second; assert the module waits according to backoff policy, retries, and ultimately returns success instead of surfacing the first error.
